news.paragraph: Daiwa Securities has raised its price target for Apple to $325 while maintaining an outperform rating, alongside reports of a competitive advantage following Samsung's delayed product launch. The company is also expanding its renewable energy capacity in India, though investors are closely monitoring how its AI strategy will meet market expectations. Recent SEC filings also noted an insider sale of shares worth approximately $369,460.
headlines: Daiwa Securities Adjusts Price Target on Apple to $325 From $310, Maintains Outperform Rating
Samsung's Late Galaxy Launch Hands Apple A Massive Advantage
Stock Market Today: Dow Jones Futures Fall, S&P 500 Gains As Trump Takes Big Tech Leaders To China—Nvidia, Nextpower, Red Cat In Focus
Apple Inc (AAPL) Partners with CleanMax to Enhance Renewable Energy Capacity in India
Apple’s India Clean Energy Push And AI Plans Test Investor Expectations
Apple Insider Sold Shares Worth $369,460, According to a Recent SEC Filing
